message:
🐛 fix(kube-chess): address Copilot review on #7903 (#7906) (#7907)

Two Copilot comments on the merged #7903 follow-up:

1. **positionKey comment was inaccurate** (line 86–88): the comment said
   the function was kept "allocation-free and O(1) per call", but the
   body does allocate — `board.map(row => row.map(...).join(''))` and
   `[...].join('')` build temporary arrays/strings. Rephrased to say we
   accept the EP geometric approximation to avoid a full legality
   simulation, and clarified that the cost is O(1) w.r.t. board size
   (not literally allocation-free).

2. **minimax double-generated legal moves per node** (line 574–587): the
   old code called `getGameResult(state)` — which internally calls
   `getAllLegalMoves` to detect checkmate/stalemate — and then called
   `getAllLegalMoves(state, state.turn)` again to drive the search.
   That doubles move-generation work at every non-terminal node.
   Refactored to call `getAllLegalMoves` once and derive the terminal
   result inline from `moves.length === 0` + `isInCheck`. Repetition
   remains unreachable inside the search (search-time `makeMove` uses
   `trackHistory=false`) so its omission is still documented as
   deliberate.

No behavior change — same terminal scoring formula, same stalemate = 0,
same repetition-unreachable invariant. Just removes redundant work and
tightens a misleading comment.

Build green; no vitest file exists for KubeChess.

Fixes #7906

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
